evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` mixes normalization/logging with optional toast emission (`notifyError`), and feature hooks call it directly (`useMediaGalleryItemState`, `useMediaGalleryHandlers`), coupling domain/feature error paths to UI notification behavior.

`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx` writes `setProjectSelectionSnapshot(...)` from a React effect to support non-React consumers, creating hidden shared-state coordination across module boundaries.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exposes `initializeSupabase()` + `getSupabaseClient()` with a required call order enforced only at runtime (catch/normalize/rethrow), indicating boot-order dependency across modules.

Task payload handling is split across multiple overlapping contracts and raw fallbacks: `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` writes `orchestrator_details` plus family/task-view contracts, while `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` rehydrates by probing `rawParams`, `orchestratorDetails`, `taskViewContract`, and `familyContract` for the same semantic fields.

`VideoTravelDetails` must read many alternate shapes (`individualSegmentParams`, `orchestratorDetails`, `taskViewContract`, `rawParams`) to derive one UI model, indicating interface ambiguity and leaky abstraction boundaries.

Editing UI contains duplicated behavior abstractions instead of one shared unit: both `InlineEditView` and `LightboxLayout` implement near-identical annotation delete/free-form floating controls with separate styling and wiring.

Credits hook access remains three-layered (`shared/hooks/useCredits` -> `shared/hooks/compat/useCredits` -> `features/billing/hooks/useCredits`), creating duplicate entry points and a persistent alias dependency edge.

The compat layer introduces governance/deprecation dependencies into runtime hook calls (`getDeprecationPolicy`, `signalPastRemovalTargetUsage`), so consumers retain coupling to migration plumbing instead of a single stable import path.

Edge guard helpers encode denial states inconsistently: `ensureRequestMethod` returns `ok:false` failure objects, while `checkRateLimit` returns `ok:true` with `value.allowed=false`, forcing different caller branching for similar gatekeeping outcomes.

Async cancellation/timing contracts are inconsistent: `waitForVariantDelay` clears prior timeout handles but leaves earlier awaiting promises unresolved, which can hang prior callers indefinitely under rapid repeated invalidations.

Scope-resolution APIs return typed status unions (`resolveVariantProjectScope`), but lineage consumers immediately convert these statuses into thrown generic errors, discarding structured status contracts at the boundary.

Multiple assigned files are explicit compatibility/deprecation shims with removal contracts (`legacySupabaseFacade.ts`, `compat/useCredits.ts`, `reportRuntimeError.ts`, `toolConstants.ts`, `components/theme/buttonVariants.ts`, `hooks/useDarkMode.ts`, `hooks/useDebounce.ts`).

Repository import scan (non-test files) found no active production imports for legacy shim paths such as `@/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabaseFacade`, `@/shared/lib/toolConstants`, `@/shared/components/theme/buttonVariants`, `@/shared/hooks/useDarkMode`, `@/shared/hooks/useDebounce`, and `@/shared/hooks/useCredits`.

`payloadBuilder.ts` still widens canonical travel input via `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput & Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams>` and actively maps deprecated fields (`structure_video_path`, `structure_video_type`, `use_uni3c`, etc.) into normalization, so old/new structure-guidance shapes still coexist.
`joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` remains a full migration adapter surface (`createJoinClipsTaskCompat`) while production call sites use canonical creation paths.

Five assigned files are compatibility entrypoints that only re-export from deeper packages (`src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts` -> `src/shared/hooks/compat/useCredits`, `src/shared/hooks/useDarkMode.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useDebounce.ts` -> `src/shared/hooks/core/*`, `src/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer.tsx` -> `src/shared/components/generation-controls/AspectRatioVisualizer`, `src/shared/components/AuthGate.tsx` -> `src/shared/auth/components/AuthGate`).

Assigned tests are still organized around legacy locations instead of canonical ownership boundaries (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts` imports `../useDarkMode`, `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts` imports `../useDebounce`), which couples test ownership to alias paths.

Holistic structure shows flat overload in both parent directories: `src/shared/hooks/` has 81 production files and `src/shared/components/` has 70 files (well above the >10 mixed-concern split threshold), while these alias files add extra top-level surface area without owning behavior.

Submission flow has two parallel composition hooks: `useFormSubmission` wires `useSubmissionOrchestrator` + `useSubmitHandler` directly, while `formSubmission/useSubmissionHandlers.ts` repeats the same wiring and appears unreferenced in the assigned scope.

State synchronization is done during render in multiple UI modules (`LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, `BrowsePresetsTab.tsx`) via `prev*Ref` checks followed by immediate `setState`, which embeds side effects in render control flow.

Lightbox/timeline orchestration has very wide prop and return surfaces (`useSharedLightboxState.ts` and `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts`), indicating coordinator hooks are carrying multiple responsibilities and high coupling.

`complete_task` contract handling is spread across modules with many fallback paths for the same fields (`params.ts` extractors and `orchestrator.ts` segment/orchestrator resolution), showing schema drift and repeated defensive parsing.

findings.summary: Shared error facade couples feature/domain code to UI toast side effects
evidence: `handleError` calls `notifyError` based on `showToast`/`appError.showToast`, so importing this shared API can trigger UI behavior.
Feature hooks call `handleError`/`reportAndRethrow` directly for operational paths, meaning non-UI logic inherits global notification policy through a cross-module utility.
findings.suggestion: Split error responsibilities: keep a pure `reportError` API in a non-UI module and move toast decisions to UI boundary adapters (for example, `useUiErrorReporter` in presentation layers). Update feature hooks to call pure reporting and have container/UI layers opt into toast behavior explicitly.

findings.summary: State/client access relies on implicit runtime initialization order across modules
evidence: `ProjectProvider` pushes selection into `projectSelectionStore` via `useEffect`, creating a separate mutable runtime channel for non-React consumers that depends on provider lifecycle timing.
`getSupabaseClient()` assumes prior `initializeSupabase()` call and fails at runtime if bootstrap order is violated.
findings.suggestion: Replace implicit init-order contracts with explicit dependency injection/composition roots: pass project selection and supabase client through typed providers or factory-created services, and remove ambient singleton access paths that can be called before initialization.

findings.summary: Travel task payload abstraction is fragmented across overlapping contracts and raw fallback reads.
evidence: `payloadBuilder` writes both top-level orchestrator fields and contract blocks (`orchestration_contract`, `task_view_contract`, `family_contract`) for the same task.
`individualTravelSegment` rebuilds state by repeatedly probing multiple sources (`rawParams`, `orchestratorDetails`, `taskViewContract`) for identical semantics like model/prompt/segment frames.
`VideoTravelDetails` repeats the same multi-source probing pattern to render details, proving the write/read contract is not singular.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical read/write schema for travel-family fields (prefer contract blocks), migrate builders/readers/UI to that schema only, and gate legacy fallback parsing behind a temporary adapter with explicit removal criteria.

findings.summary: Annotation floating controls are duplicated between inline editor and lightbox layout.
evidence: `InlineEditView` defines `AnnotationButtons` with selected-shape lookup, free-form toggle, and delete positioning logic.
`LightboxLayout` defines a separate `AnnotationButtons` with the same interaction model and near-identical control semantics.
Behavioral parity is maintained by duplication, increasing drift risk when annotation interaction changes.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared annotation-control component/hook (position resolution + free-form/delete actions + button rendering variants) and consume it in both inline and lightbox surfaces.

findings.summary: Credits hook compatibility alias chain keeps redundant dependency paths active.
evidence: `useCredits.ts` is a pure re-export layer pointing to `compat/useCredits`.
`compat/useCredits` forwards to `features/billing/hooks/useCredits` while adding governance/deprecation runtime calls, preserving multiple valid import paths for the same dependency.
Removal target metadata exists, but the alias remains first-class and continues to add cross-module coupling.
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration to `@/features/billing/hooks/useCredits`, remove `shared/hooks/useCredits` entrypoint, and restrict any remaining deprecation signaling to compile-time or dev-only tooling rather than runtime hook wrappers.

findings.summary: Debounced variant invalidation can leave earlier callers awaiting forever
evidence: `waitForVariantDelay` stores one timeout per `generationId`; when a second call arrives it `clearTimeout(existingTimeout)` and deletes the map entry, but the first call's `new Promise` resolve path is never triggered.
Because `invalidateVariantChange` awaits `waitForVariantDelay`, earlier callers can hang indefinitely after being superseded by newer calls.
`runTimelineWriteWithTimeout` in `timelineWriteQueue.ts` shows the opposite contract style: timeout/cancellation paths always settle (throw or return), so callers do not hang.
findings.suggestion: Change variant-delay dedupe to store and settle waiter state, not just timeout IDs. Keep a per-key record with timeout + resolver, and on supersession either resolve prior waiters immediately (coalesced) or reject them with a typed cancellation error so every call to `invalidateVariantChange` settles.

findings.summary: Edge request gates use conflicting denial/result shapes for similar control flow
evidence: `ensureRequestMethod` returns `operationFailure(...errorCode:'method_not_allowed')` (`ok:false`) for denied requests.
`checkRateLimit` returns `operationSuccess({ allowed:false, ... })` (`ok:true`) when a request is denied by policy, but also returns `ok:true` in degraded fail-open error scenarios.
Other edge helpers such as billing `triggerCostCalculation` model non-success states as `ok:false` failures, so callers cannot apply one consistent gate-handling pattern across shared utilities.
findings.suggestion: Standardize gatekeeper contracts: either (A) all deny states return `OperationFailure` with explicit codes (`rate_limit_exceeded`, `method_not_allowed`), or (B) all gatekeepers return a dedicated `GateDecision` type separate from `OperationResult`. Document and enforce one pattern across `_shared` edge helpers.

findings.summary: Typed scope-status API is erased into generic thrown errors in lineage APIs
evidence: `resolveVariantProjectScope` returns a rich union status (`missing_variant`, `scope_mismatch`, `missing_project_scope`, etc.) with optional `queryError`.
`fetchVariantById` in `useLineageChain.ts` converts non-`ok` statuses into thrown generic `Error` messages via `handleError`, dropping structured status information from the public lineage surface.
Consumers of `useLineageChain` get only `error: Error | null` and cannot branch reliably on scope/ownership failure categories that the repository layer already defines.
findings.suggestion: Preserve typed failure categories across the lineage boundary: return a discriminated result (`{ok:false, status,...}`) from lineage fetch paths or wrap errors in a typed `LineageScopeError` carrying repository status codes so UI callers can handle `scope_mismatch`/`missing_variant` deterministically.

findings.summary: Several migration shims remain in assigned files even though production imports appear already migrated, leaving dead compatibility surfaces.
evidence: Each file is explicitly marked as deprecated/compatibility surface with migration guidance and removal gates.
Non-test import scan found no active runtime imports for these legacy module paths; hits were limited to shim self-references or tests.
This indicates migration appears complete in runtime code, but cleanup has not happened.
findings.suggestion: Delete unused shim modules and replace remaining compatibility tests with canonical-path tests. Keep a short-lived changelog entry if needed, but remove proxy/deprecation wrappers once import graph confirms no runtime consumers.

findings.summary: Travel-between-images payload building still supports deprecated structure-video fields, preserving mixed API shapes past migration.
evidence: `legacyStructureVideo.ts` defines deprecated fields (`structure_video_path`, `structure_video_motion_strength`, `structure_video_type`, `use_uni3c`) as active compatibility contract.
`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` consumes `Partial<LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams>` and passes legacy keys into structure guidance normalization.
This keeps read/write migration boundary open rather than forcing canonical `structure_guidance` + `structure_videos` input shape.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a strict canonical input mode in payload builder (default on), gate legacy-field acceptance behind an explicit transitional flag, and schedule removal of `LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` from the builder signature.

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility adapter remains as maintained migration code despite canonical task creation paths being used in production.
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still provides broad legacy field translation and removal-target enforcement logic.
Production call sites use canonical join-clips APIs; compat adapter references are absent outside tests and the module itself.
Maintaining unused compat translation logic increases migration debt and future maintenance overhead.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate and remove `createJoinClipsTaskCompat` after updating compatibility tests to canonical input contracts; if short-term retention is required, isolate it under a test-only or explicit migration package boundary.

findings.summary: Legacy top-level alias files duplicate canonical package entrypoints and blur ownership.
evidence: Each file is a pass-through re-export with no local behavior, but is placed in high-traffic top-level directories (`shared/hooks`, `shared/components`) instead of the domain-owned package.

`useCredits.ts` points to `shared/hooks/compat/useCredits` (which itself bridges to `features/billing/hooks/useCredits`), while UI aliases point into `shared/auth/components` and `shared/components/generation-controls`.
This creates two importable homes per module, so package boundaries are discoverability-ambiguous and migrations stay partially complete.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Define canonical homes as `src/shared/hooks/core/*`, `src/features/billing/hooks/*`, `src/shared/components/generation-controls/*`, and `src/shared/auth/components/*` in an ADR/readme note. 2) Update imports in consumers to canonical paths first (`rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useDarkMode|@/shared/hooks/useDebounce|@/shared/hooks/useCredits|@/shared/components/AuthGate|@/shared/components/AspectRatioVisualizer" src` then replace). 3) Keep temporary aliases only in one dedicated legacy folder (`src/shared/legacy/aliases/*`) with removal dates, then remove current top-level alias files. 4) Validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/components/BulkEditControls.test.tsx`, and a zero-usage check using the same `rg` patterns.

findings.summary: Hook tests are organized under legacy alias locations instead of canonical module directories.
evidence: `useDarkMode.test.ts` imports `../useDarkMode` and `useDebounce.test.ts` imports `../useDebounce`, which tests alias entrypoints rather than canonical files now in `src/shared/hooks/core/`.
Because the alias files are migration shims, colocating tests under `shared/hooks/__tests__` preserves obsolete package boundaries and makes canonical ownership less obvious.

The same pattern appears in component alias tests outside this batch (`shared/components/__tests__/AuthGate.test.tsx`, `shared/components/__tests__/AspectRatioVisualizer.test.tsx`), indicating systemic organization drift.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Move tests to canonical owners (`git mv src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/core/useDarkMode.test.ts` and `git mv src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/hooks/core/useDebounce.test.ts`; keep `useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts` with its real module if it remains in `shared/hooks`). 2) Update imports to local canonical paths (`./useDarkMode`, `./useDebounce`). 3) Add a temporary compatibility smoke test only where alias contracts are still required, then retire it with the alias removal. 4) Validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/core/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/core/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts` and `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Generation/shot contracts remain split across two active type surfaces.
evidence: `README.md` states `@/domains/generation/types/public` is canonical and legacy shot aliasing is removed.

`public.ts` only re-exports domain types, but structure data shows `src/types/` still hosts parallel generation/shot contract files with non-trivial fan-in (`avg_fan_in: 4.7`) and active imports from shared layers.
findings.suggestion: Complete the contract convergence by deprecating direct generation/shot exports in `src/types/*` and funneling all generation/shot type imports through `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts`. Stage it: (1) add temporary re-export shims in `src/types/*` that point to domain types, (2) bulk-rewrite imports to the canonical domain barrel (`rg "@/types/(generation|shot|generationAndShots)" src` then codemod), (3) remove old files once usage is zero, (4) validate with `npm run check:shots-shim-usage && npm run check:contracts && npm run typecheck`.

findings.summary: Root README is overloaded with package-specific governance policy details.
evidence: `README.md` contains detailed operational policy for Supabase bootstrap, legacy shim gates, error handling ownership, and contract enforcement commands.

These policies map to specific boundary packages (`src/integrations/supabase/*`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/*`) rather than repository-root concerns, so ownership is separated from implementation location.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged docs reorg so policy ownership matches package boundaries: (1) create `docs/governance/contracts/` with `supabase-runtime.md`, `error-handling.md`, and `compatibility-shims.md`; (2) move contract details out of `README.md` into those files, keeping README as index-level summary with links; (3) in each package (`src/integrations/supabase/`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/`, `src/domains/generation/types/`), add a short local `README.md` linking to the authoritative governance doc; (4) update references via `rg "check:legacy-supabase-usage|check:error-runtime-alias-usage|check:shots-shim-usage" README.md docs -n`; (5) validate link and policy consistency with `npm run quality:check`.

findings.summary: Form submission orchestration is duplicated by an unused pass-through hook
evidence: `useFormSubmission` composes `useSubmissionOrchestrator` + `useSubmitHandler` and returns queue handlers directly.
`useSubmissionHandlers` repeats the same dependency bag and orchestration wiring, adding only pass-through indirection.
Within assigned files, `useSubmissionHandlers` has no callsites, so the duplicate abstraction increases maintenance surface without behavior.
findings.suggestion: Remove `useSubmissionHandlers` (or replace `useFormSubmission` with it, but keep only one composition entrypoint). Keep a single exported hook for submission flow and move shared queue wiring to one tested helper if needed.

findings.summary: Components mutate state during render to sync filters, obscuring control flow
evidence: `LoraSelectorModal` compares refs in render (`prevLoraTypeRef`, `prevValidOptionsRef`) and calls `setSelectedModelFilter`/`setSelectedSubFilter` inside render branches.
`BrowsePresetsTab` uses a render-time `prevFilterKeyRef` check and calls `setPage(0)` in render when filter key changes.
Both components encode synchronization side effects in render rather than event handlers/effects, which makes render behavior stateful and harder to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Move these resets to explicit transitions: either derive values from props without local mirror state, or use `useEffect`/event-driven reducers that react to dependency changes. Keep render pure and side-effect free.

findings.summary: Lightbox/timeline orchestrators are oversized coordination hubs with broad prop bags
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState` composes many concern-specific interfaces into one large `UseSharedLightboxStateProps` and returns a deeply nested mega-object covering variants, navigation, star, references, lineage, shots, source generation, layout, and button-group wiring.

`ImageLightbox` must construct and pass a very large argument object into `useSharedLightboxState`, mixing navigation, shot workflows, edit-mode flags, media calculations, and action handlers in one callsite.
`useTimelineOrchestrator` combines drag/drop, zoom, endpoint drag, selection, pending-state, prefetching, structure-video placement, and device behavior in a single 600+ LOC coordinator hook.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration by stable bounded contexts (e.g., navigation, variants, shot actions, edit-mode state, structure-video timeline). Introduce thin composition layers that pass smaller typed contracts between sub-hooks instead of one wide omniscient input/output API.

findings.summary: Complete-task pipeline relies on scattered fallback extraction instead of one canonical schema
evidence: `params.ts` explicitly supports multiple historical paths for the same semantic fields (`run_id`, `based_on`, `shot_id`, `add_in_position`) via repeated path lists.
`orchestrator.ts` includes additional multi-source resolution logic (`resolveExpectedSegmentCount`, orchestrator lookup fallback paths) to recover missing/shifted payload shape.
`index.ts` must coordinate mode-specific checks and security/auth/orchestrator/billing branches around these variable shapes, indicating contract normalization is not centralized early.
findings.suggestion: Define a versioned canonical completion payload contract and normalize once at the boundary (request parse stage). Downstream modules should consume a single typed normalized object and drop path-by-path fallback extraction.
